context: The agent is asked to draft a README.md and CHANGELOG.md for a new uinaf-branded CLI. This scenario tests whether the skill correctly applies the cross-surface voice rules and the content-track structural patterns (README shape, changelog format) without leaning on the Tailwind / web subset of the system.

checklist.name: README title and tagline
checklist.description: README.md opens with `# tccutil` (lowercase title, no quotes, no decoration) followed by a single literal one-line tagline directly below describing what the tool does. Tagline is lowercase, ends with a period, and contains no benefit / marketing language.

checklist.name: README install block at top
checklist.description: A single fenced ```bash code block sits near the top of the README, containing both the brew install line and at least one minimal usage invocation (e.g. `tccutil reset Camera` or `tccutil list`). The block is not preceded by 'Installation:' / 'Getting started:' headings.

checklist.name: README sections match the pattern
checklist.description: Section headings present include `## why`, `## docs`, `## license`. Headings are lowercase. No `## features`, `## highlights`, `## benefits`, or `## getting started` sections appear.

checklist.name: README docs link uses ↗
checklist.description: The docs link points at `uinaf.dev/tccutil` and is rendered as a markdown link with a trailing `↗` glyph inside the link text (e.g. `[uinaf.dev/tccutil ↗](https://uinaf.dev/tccutil)`).

checklist.name: README license one-liner
checklist.description: The `## license` section is one line containing `MIT`. The token `MIT` is the only uppercase string allowed in body content. No copyright boilerplate is duplicated.

checklist.name: README length
checklist.description: README body content (excluding code blocks) is under ~25 lines. No filler paragraphs, no 'about uinaf' section, no contributor-handbook content.

checklist.name: CHANGELOG newest-first, dated
checklist.description: CHANGELOG.md is headed `# changelog` (lowercase) and lists at least two entries newest-first. Each entry header is `## YYYY-MM-DD` or `## v0.1.0 — 2026-04-25` (em-dash, lowercase v). The 2026-04-25 entry appears above the 2026-04-22 entry.

checklist.name: CHANGELOG bullet shape
checklist.description: Each entry body is a `-` bullet list. Each bullet is lowercase, ends with a period, and is one short clause. No nested bullets, no multi-line bullets, no inline links to PRs unless minimal.

checklist.name: CHANGELOG no categories or emoji
checklist.description: No `### Added`, `### Changed`, `### Fixed`, `### Deprecated`, or similar category subheadings. No emoji prefixes (no 🐛 ✨ 🎉 ⚡). No bracketed tags like `[BREAKING]`.

checklist.name: Lowercase voice across both files
checklist.description: Every visible string in both files is lowercase. Exceptions allowed: code identifiers (`tccutil`, `Camera`, `bundle-id`), URLs, file paths, the literal token `MIT` in the license section, and shell command tokens inside fenced code blocks. No Title Case, no sentence-case capitalization of headings, no all-caps.

checklist.name: No emoji, no exclamation marks
checklist.description: Both files contain zero emoji and zero exclamation marks anywhere — body, headings, code comments included.

checklist.name: No SaaS marketing language
checklist.description: Neither file contains any of: `empower`, `unlock`, `synergies`, `simply`, `just`, `essentially`, `transform`, `elevate`, `mission`, `seamless`, `revolutionary`, `cutting-edge`, `leverage`, `harness`, `delight`. Tagline and `## why` paragraph describe what the tool literally does, not benefits.

checklist.name: Voice — sentence fragments with periods, terse
checklist.description: Sentences and bullets read terse and dry. Fragments end with periods. No paragraphs longer than 3 sentences. No exclamation marks. No 'You will love…' / 'We're excited to…' / 'Built for…' phrasings.

checklist.name: External links carry ↗
checklist.description: Every external (off-domain) link in both files carries a trailing `↗` (U+2197) glyph inside the link text. Internal repo paths and inline code do not need the arrow.

checklist.name: No banned UI scaffolding leaked into content
checklist.description: Neither file contains HTML tags, Tailwind classes, `<div>` / `<section>`, badge images (`![](https://shields.io/...)` etc), or any web-component reference. README and CHANGELOG are pure markdown text + fenced code blocks.
